标题: AgentFloor: How Far Up the Tool Use Ladder Can Small Open-Weight Models Go?
原文摘要: This paper evaluates how far small open-weight language models can progress in tool-use tasks, focusing on whether lightweight models can reliably plan, call tools, and recover from errors in agentic workflows.
热点理由: 小模型能否承担 agent 工具调用，是 2026 年降本和端侧部署的重要问题

标题: Odysseus: Scaling VLMs to 100+ Turn Decision-Making in Games via Reinforcement Learning
原文摘要: The paper studies long-horizon decision-making for vision-language models in game environments, using reinforcement learning to push VLM agents toward 100+ turn planning and action execution.
热点理由: 长程决策、多模态 agent、RL 三个方向叠加，适合技术解读

标题: ResRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning via Negative Sample Projection Residual Reinforcement Learning
原文摘要: ResRL proposes a reinforcement learning method that improves LLM reasoning by using negative sample projection and residual learning, aiming to make reasoning optimization more stable and sample-efficient.
热点理由: ICML 2026 相关 reasoning RL 论文，贴近后训练和推理增强热点

标题: Rethinking LLM Ensembling from the Perspective of Mixture Models
原文摘要: This work reinterprets LLM ensembling through mixture model theory, discussing when model combination improves robustness, diversity, uncertainty estimation, and downstream performance.
热点理由: ICML 2026 Spotlight，适合做模型融合、路由、MoE 相关选题

标题: LLM-Oriented Information Retrieval: A Denoising-First Perspective
原文摘要: The paper argues that retrieval for LLMs should be designed differently from retrieval for humans, emphasizing denoising, attention budget, hallucination prevention, and agentic search quality.
热点理由: RAG 从“召回更多”转向“给 LLM 去噪”，非常适合做产品和技术选题

标题: When RAG Chatbots Expose Their Backend: An Anonymized Case Study of Privacy and Security Risks in Patient-Facing Medical AI
原文摘要: This case study analyzes privacy and security risks in patient-facing medical RAG chatbots, especially how retrieval systems may expose backend data, system structure, or sensitive content.
热点理由: 医疗 RAG、安全、隐私三个关键词叠加，适合转化成风险分析内容

标题: RunAgent: Interpreting Natural-Language Plans with Constraint-Guided Execution
原文摘要: RunAgent studies how natural-language plans can be interpreted and executed under explicit constraints, aiming to make agent execution more controllable, verifiable, and less prone to unsafe behavior.
热点理由: 从 prompt 到可控执行，是 agent 走向生产系统的关键问题

标题: Can Coding Agents Reproduce Findings in Computational Materials Science?
原文摘要: This paper evaluates whether coding agents can reproduce scientific computational results, using materials science as a testbed for code understanding, experiment execution, and result validation.
热点理由: coding agent 不只是写网页，而是开始进入科研复现和自动实验

标题: Graph Query Generation with Constraint-guided Large Language Agents
原文摘要: The paper explores how large language agents can generate graph queries under structural and semantic constraints, targeting more reliable database and knowledge graph interaction.
热点理由: Agent + 数据库 + 约束生成，是企业 AI 落地中的高频技术栈

标题: SURE-RAG: Sufficiency and Uncertainty-Aware Evidence Verification for Selective Retrieval-Augmented Generation
原文摘要: SURE-RAG introduces sufficiency and uncertainty-aware evidence verification for selective RAG, helping systems decide when retrieved evidence is enough and when generation should be deferred or revised.
热点理由: RAG 可靠性从召回质量进入证据充分性和不确定性判断阶段

标题: CuraView: A Multi-Agent Framework for Medical Hallucination Detection with GraphRAG-Enhanced Knowledge Verification
原文摘要: CuraView proposes a multi-agent medical hallucination detection framework, combining GraphRAG-based verification with role-specialized agents for clinical answer checking.
热点理由: 多智能体 + 医疗幻觉检测 + GraphRAG，具备强应用场景

标题: Detecting Stealth Sycophancy in Mental-Health Dialogue with Dynamic Emotional Signature Graphs
原文摘要: This paper studies stealth sycophancy in mental-health dialogue systems, using dynamic emotional signature graphs to detect when models subtly over-agree or reinforce harmful user framing.
热点理由: 心理健康 AI 中的 sycophancy 是真实产品风险，不只是抽象安全问题

标题: When to Think, When to Speak: Learning Disclosure Policies for LLM Reasoning
原文摘要: The paper investigates when LLMs should reveal reasoning, when they should suppress internal reasoning, and how disclosure policies affect trust, safety, and task performance.
热点理由: ICML 2026 论文，切中“模型该不该展示推理过程”的核心产品与安全问题

标题: Sparse Memory Finetuning as a Low-Forgetting Alternative to LoRA and Full Finetuning
原文摘要: This paper proposes sparse memory finetuning as an alternative to LoRA and full finetuning, aiming to reduce catastrophic forgetting while preserving adaptation efficiency.
热点理由: LoRA 后的微调替代路线仍是模型应用团队关注点

标题: Geometric Deviation as an Unsupervised Pre-Generation Reliability Signal: Probing LLM Representations for Answerability
原文摘要: The work proposes a representation-level reliability signal before generation, using geometric deviation to estimate whether an LLM is likely to answer reliably.
热点理由: 生成前可靠性判断，比事后 hallucination detection 更适合产品化

标题: MCJudgeBench: A Benchmark for Constraint-Level Judge Evaluation in Multi-Constraint Instruction Following
原文摘要: MCJudgeBench evaluates judge models at the constraint level, focusing on whether LLM-as-a-judge systems can correctly assess complex multi-constraint instruction following.
热点理由: Agent 和 LLM 应用越多，judge 模型的可靠性评估越重要

标题: TRACE: A Metrologically-Grounded Engineering Framework for Trustworthy Agentic AI Systems in Operationally Critical Domains
原文摘要: TRACE proposes an engineering framework for trustworthy agentic AI in operationally critical domains, emphasizing measurement, verification, traceability, and system-level accountability.
热点理由: agent 进入关键业务后，工程化可信框架会成为刚需

标题: Steer Like the LLM: Activation Steering that Mimics Prompting
原文摘要: The paper studies activation steering methods that mimic prompting behavior, exploring whether internal representation control can reproduce or improve prompt-based model steering.
热点理由: ICML 2026 方向，连接 prompt engineering、可解释性和模型内部控制

标题: Atomic Fact-Checking Increases Clinician Trust in Large Language Model Recommendations for Oncology Decision Support: A Randomized Controlled Trial
原文摘要: This randomized controlled trial studies whether atomic fact-checking can increase clinician trust in LLM-generated oncology decision support recommendations.
热点理由: 医疗 LLM 不只是准确率问题，而是医生是否信任和如何验证的问题

标题: The Counterexample Game: Iterated Conceptual Analysis and Repair in Language Models
原文摘要: The paper introduces a counterexample-driven game for conceptual analysis and repair in language models, using iterative counterexamples to expose and fix conceptual errors.
热点理由: 用反例驱动模型修正，适合转成 reasoning、eval 和自我改进相关内容

标题: CC-OCR V2: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models for Literacy in Real-world Document Processing
原文摘要: CC-OCR V2 benchmarks large multimodal models on real-world document processing, focusing on literacy, OCR robustness, layout understanding, and practical document AI scenarios.
热点理由: 多模态模型从看图走向文档理解，是企业级 AI 应用高频需求
